codeReviewIssues.description: charge() u128→u64 truncation: `(elapsed_ns as u128 * DEFAULT_WEIGHT as u128 / self.weight as u128) as u64` silently truncates if the intermediate result exceeds u64::MAX. For weight=1 (lowest possible), this occurs at elapsed > ~208 days in a single charge call. For default weight (1024), delta equals elapsed so no truncation. Practically unreachable since charge() is called per-tick (~ms intervals), but a saturating cast (e.g., `min(result, u64::MAX as u128) as u64`) would be strictly correct. Same pattern in virtual_deadline() but requested_slice is bounded by realistic values so no practical risk there either.

summary: Thorough audit of 4 scheduling files. All 4 unsafe blocks in scheduler.rs have verified SAFETY comments (swap_ttbr0 TLB sequence, block_current_unless_woken aliasing bypass, init/init_secondary TPIDR_EL1 writes). Idle thread park fix (Fix 1) re-verified sound — idle threads correctly return to their core's slot, never enqueued. Two real overflow bugs found and fixed in scheduling_algorithm.rs: charge() multiplication overflow and virtual_deadline() addition overflow, both now use u128 intermediates + saturating_add. TDD discipline followed — tests demonstrated failures before fixes. per_core.rs SMP isolation verified: AtomicBool with Release/Acquire ordering, no cross-core data races. scheduling_context.rs fully verified: all saturating arithmetic, validate_params prevents division-by-zero, state transitions complete. 14 new tests (8 EEVDF overflow/edge-case, 6 scheduling context), 401 total pass. VAL-SCHED-001 (scheduler unsafe blocks), VAL-SCHED-003 (EEVDF + per-core), and VAL-SCHED-004 (new tests) all satisfied. One non-blocking finding: u128→u64 truncation in charge() could theoretically under-charge for extreme elapsed values (>208 days per tick), but this is practically unreachable.
